The plan includes sample 500 parallel sentence pairs for each source language (non-English) and target language (English) from News Commentary v16 (or CCAligned if unavailable).

The plan includes tokenize each parallel sentence pair and extract hidden states of the last token from all layers of the LLM (e.g., BLOOMZ-7B1-MT), yielding vectors h^s_{i,l} and h^t_{i,l} for every layer l.
The plan includes for each layer l, stack the extracted source and target hidden states across all N pairs to form matrices H_s and H_t.
The plan includes compute the optimal alignment matrix W_l^* = (H_s^T H_s)^{-1} (H_s^T H_t) using a closed-form least-squares solver.
The plan includes store all learned alignment matrices {W_l^*} for l = 1 to L (all layers).
The plan includes during inference on a test input in source language, feed the sequence and capture the hidden state h^s_{q,l} of the last token at each layer l.
The plan includes for each layer l, project the source hidden state using the alignment matrix: ŷ^t_{q,l} = W_l^* h^s_{q,l}.
The plan includes blend the projected target state with the original hidden state using intervention strength α: h^{mix}_{q,l} = h^s_{q,l} + α ŷ^t_{q,l}.
The plan includes replace the last token's hidden state at layer l with h^{mix}_{q,l} and continue decoding.
The plan includes tune α per source language using a validation set by sweeping over the interval [-1, 1] to maximise task-specific performance.
The plan includes evaluate the intervened model on downstream multilingual benchmarks (discriminative tasks: accuracy; generative tasks: EM, BLEU, accuracy).
The plan includes the setting 'INCLINE-HIDDEN': Intervene on hidden states of all layers, aligning source to target (English) using learned alignment matrices.
The plan includes the setting 'INCLINE-ATTN': Intervene on outputs of attention heads.
The plan includes the setting 'INCLINE-FFN': Intervene on outputs of FFN blocks.
The plan includes the setting 'INCLINE-EMB': Intervene on embeddings.
The plan includes the setting 'INCLINE-FDEV': INCLINE with alignment matrices trained on high-quality Flores development set instead of News Commentary.
The plan includes the setting 'SFT + INCLINE': Apply INCLINE on top of a task-specific supervised fine-tuned model (English training data).
The plan includes the use of the News Commentary v16 dataset.
The plan includes the use of the CCAligned dataset.
The plan includes the use of the Flores development set dataset.

The plan includes the Accuracy evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Exact Match (EM) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the BLEU evaluation metric.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'α (intervention strength)' with value range [-1, 1], per-language optimal value determined by validation (e.g., positive for Spanish, negative for Chinese).
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'Number of parallel sentences for training alignment' with value 500.

source.ref: Number of parallel sentences for training alignment

description: The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'Target component' with value Hidden states (INCLINE-HIDDEN) as default; also explored attention, FFN, embeddings.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'Intervention position' with value Last token only.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'Layers intervened' with value All layers.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: INCLINE significantly improves accuracy on discriminative tasks (XCOPA, XStoryCloze, XWinograd, XCSQA, XNLI) over BASELINE and other intervention baselines, with up to +4.96 average gain on XStoryCloze and clear improvements on both seen and unseen languages. SFT+INCLINE further boosts SFT performance.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: INCLINE enhances generative task performance (MZsRE, Flores, WMT23, MGSM) over baselines, achieving best BLEU on Flores and WMT23, improving EM on MZsRE by +3.26, and accuracy on MGSM by +3.50.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: Intervening on hidden states (INCLINE-HIDDEN) yields the greatest average performance gains across XCOPA, XCSQA, Flores, MGSM compared to intervening on attention, FFN, or embeddings.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4: INCLINE consistently improves XStoryCloze accuracy across five different LLM backbones (BLOOMZ-7B1-MT, LLAMA3.1-8B-INSTRUCT, LLAMA2-7B-CHAT, MISTRAL-7B-INSTRUCT, FALCON-7B-INSTRUCT).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 5: Training INCLINE alignment matrices with high-quality parallel sentences (Flores dev, INCLINE-FDEV) further improves BLEU on Flores over using News Commentary.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: INCLINE achieves near-optimal accuracy with 500 parallel sentences and only 172 seconds training time; increasing data further does not improve accuracy, showing cost-effectiveness.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: INCLINE intervention increases Correct Prediction Consistency (CPC) between non-English and English on XStoryCloze, especially for low-resource Swahili (from 0.54 to 0.65).

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: Optimal intervention strength α differs by language: positive for Spanish (Indo-European like English) and negative for Chinese (Sino-Tibetan), suggesting language relatedness influences α.
The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 5: Larger BLOOMZ models benefit more from INCLINE on MZsRE; relative improvement over baseline increases with model size.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 5: INCLINE further improves Exact Match on MZsRE when combined with in-context learning (0-shot, 2-shot, 4-shot), demonstrating complementary benefits.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 1): INCLINE significantly improves discriminative task performance over BASELINE and intervention baselines (ITI, CAA), e.g., +4.96 average accuracy on XStoryCloze.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 2): INCLINE improves generative task metrics (EM, BLEU, accuracy) over baselines, including +3.50 accuracy on MGSM and best BLEU on Flores/WMT23.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Figure 3): INCLINE is cost-effective: training alignment matrices requires only 500 parallel sentences and 172 seconds on one A100.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 3): Intervening on hidden states is superior to intervening on attention, FFN, or embeddings across tasks.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 4): INCLINE generalizes across diverse LLM backbones, improving XStoryCloze accuracy for all five tested models.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 5): Using high-quality parallel data (Flores dev) for training alignment matrices yields better performance than standard News Commentary parallel data.
